sutta: The Buddha said it is impossible monks it cannot come to pass that in one world system at one at the end and the same time there should arise two arahants who are fully enlightened once sama sam Buddha but monks it is quite possible for a single arahant a fully enlightened one to arise
commentary: these few suttas here concerning the impossible are also found in majima nikaya number and this particular sutta saying that you cannot find two Buddhas sama sambudas at the same time in one world system is another one of those suttas that seem to be in conflict with the mahayana sutras for example in the earth store bodhisattva they say that many Buddhas came when the Buddha started to preach in fact you can find the diamond sutra in the other sutras also contradicting uh this uh straw bodhisattva sutra because in the diamond sutra it is said that after a Buddha has passed into mahaparinibana if you expect to see the Buddha again or hear his voice or touch him etc then the diamond sutra says that you don't understand the Dhamma and you have gone in along the deviant path in other words once a Buddha has passed into nibana you can never see him again you can never touch him again hear his voice etc so there's one thing if you uh if you investigate you find that there are certain sutras that contradict each other it is only in the original four earliest suttas in the pali suitors and the four nikayas that you find they are very consistent that there is no contradiction one sutta:point one point fifteen point twelve the Buddha said it is impossible monks it cannot come to pass that a woman should be an arahant who is a samasan Buddha but monks it is quite possible for a man to be one and then uh sudan number 13 14 15 and 16 and the. Buddha says it is impossible it cannot come to pass that a woman should be a universal monarch or so here we find these few suttas there are five things that a woman cannot become become a samasan Buddha cannot become a universal king who rules over the whole world cannot become cannot become Māra or satan cannot become brahma and this again you find there's a little contradiction in the lotus sutra where it is mentioned that a woman can become masama sambuda here also it shows that there is a little difference between a man and a woman sometimes we like to think that a man and a woman are the same but there is some difference
